title: Methodology
subtitle: How the Brazil Elections 2026 Portal Works

disclaimer.heading: Independence Statement
disclaimer.body: This portal is an independent project with no party affiliation or electoral funding. There is no editorial intervention of any kind: articles are collected automatically from public sources, summaries are generated by artificial intelligence, and sentiment analyses are derived algorithmically. The complete source code is available for public consultation and audit at github.com/carlosduplar/eleicoes-2026-monitor.

collection.label: Collection
collection.body: RSS from 20+ outlets and scraping of party websites and polling institutes, every 10 minutes via GitHub Actions.

summarization.label: Summarization
summarization.body: Open-source language models with hierarchical fallback: NVIDIA NIM (free) -> OpenRouter (free, 200 req/day) -> Ollama Cloud (free) -> Vertex AI Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite (paid priority) -> MiMo V2 Flash.

sentiment.label: Sentiment
sentiment.body: Algorithmic analysis of news tone. Scores reflect the tone of headlines and summaries -- not an editorial evaluation or opinion poll.

polls.label: Election Polls
polls.body: Automatically collected from polling institute websites. Reproduced without editorial modification.

quiz.label: Affinity Quiz
quiz.body: Positions extracted from verified statements in collected news. Sources shown in results only -- never during questions.

limitations.heading: Known limitations
items: AI models can make interpretation errors.
Source coverage may have availability bias -- outlets without RSS are not included.
Candidate positions evolve during the campaign; the pipeline updates daily.
Sentiment analysis is not equivalent to opinion polling.

error_reporting.heading: Report errors
error_reporting.body: Found an error? Open an issue on the public repository.
